documents.title: System Architecture

documents.notes: Comprehensive and well-organized. The tool count discrepancy is because core tools (complete, request_user_input) are counted in source but not always in the doc table. Needs a consistent counting policy.

Missing analytics tools section entirely (build_analytics_context, build_sql_query, validate_sql_query, execute_sql_query)

User: No screenshot-based walkthrough of the Web UI

Admin: No disaster recovery procedures beyond backup/restore

No upgrade/migration procedures for database schema changes
No log aggregation setup guide (ELK, CloudWatch, etc.)

critical_issues_summary.issue: Health endpoint path inconsistency: QUICKSTART.md, QUICK_REFERENCE.md, and API_REFERENCE.md reference /api/chat/health which does not exist. Actual endpoints are /health/live and /health/ready. Users following the Quickstart will get a 404.

FastAPI entry point path wrong: Server README and QUICK_REFERENCE list starboard_server.api.main:app but actual entry point is starboard_server.main:app (confirmed from source). Developers cannot start the server with the documented command.

API Reference is significantly inaccurate: endpoint paths inconsistent (/api/chat/ vs /api/conversations/ vs /api/v2/), some documented endpoints may not exist (visualization, template, query resolution), and tool counts are wrong. Should be regenerated from OpenAPI spec.
Tool Catalog is 3 months stale and missing 3 entire tool categories (Analytics, Discovery, Warehouse detail). Tool counts are wrong. This is the primary developer reference for tool implementations.
env.example uses deprecated domain names ('table', 'compute') in DISABLED_AGENT_DOMAINS comment instead of current names ('uc', 'cluster'). Could confuse administrators.
